---
title: "Init"
---

Initialize a new Xorq project from a template.

## Usage

```bash
xorq init [OPTIONS]
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-p`, `--path` | `./xorq-template` | Directory where the template is created. |
| `-t`, `--template` | `cached-fetcher` | Template to scaffold. One of `cached-fetcher`, `sklearn`, `penguins`. |
| `-b`, `--branch` | per-template default | Branch (or commit) of the template repo to check out. |

## Templates

- **`cached-fetcher`**—Default template for cached data-fetching workflows.
- **`sklearn`**—Template for ML workflows with scikit-learn.
- **`penguins`**—Penguins dataset example pipeline.

Each template has a default branch (a pinned commit) that gets checked out
unless `--branch` is supplied.

## Examples

Initialize with the default template:
```bash
xorq init
```

Initialize in a custom directory:
```bash
xorq init --path ./my-project
```

Initialize with the sklearn template:
```bash
xorq init --template sklearn --path ./ml-project
```

Pin to a specific branch of the template repo:
```bash
xorq init --template penguins --branch main --path ./penguins-demo
```

## Output

The command creates a new directory with template files and prints:
```
initialized xorq template `{template}` to {path}
```
